title: Procurement Contract MVP Fused Ontology
description: Unified ontology fused from code, DDL, sample data and procurement documents.

concepts.name: Department
concepts.description: Organizational department that owns users and purchase requests.

concepts.name: User
concepts.description: System or organizational actor with a role code and department.

concepts.name: Supplier
concepts.description: External vendor-like party that provides goods or services and is checked for eligibility and risk.

concepts.name: Purchase Request
concepts.description: Request for goods or services before sourcing, contracting, purchase order release, receiving, invoicing and payment.

concepts.name: Purchase Request Line
concepts.description: Line item under a purchase request; item/material semantics appear as line attributes rather than a separate material master concept.

concepts.name: Approval Record
concepts.description: Approval decision record for purchase request, contract or payment request targets; target relationship is polymorphic.

concepts.name: Contract
concepts.description: Legal agreement linked to a purchase request and supplier, with lifecycle and legal review information.

concepts.name: Purchase Order
concepts.description: Operational order issued under a contract and related purchase request, distinct from purchase request.

concepts.name: Goods Receipt
concepts.description: Receiving evidence for delivered goods or services against a purchase order.

concepts.name: Invoice
concepts.description: Supplier billing document matched against purchase order and goods receipt before payment.

concepts.name: Payment Request
concepts.description: Finance request or application to pay a matched invoice.

attributes.name: department_id
attributes.description: Department primary identifier.

attributes.name: department_code
attributes.description: Unique department code.

attributes.name: role_code
attributes.description: Role code value; DEPT_MANAGER is the stored value while documents name Department Manager.

attributes.name: supplier_status
attributes.description: Code explicitly checks ACTIVE; DDL and sample also show SUSPENDED and PENDING_REVIEW.

attributes.name: risk_rating
attributes.description: Code checks HIGH as ineligible; DDL and sample show LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH.

attributes.name: request_no
attributes.description: Human-readable PR number; also represented as requestNo in code and pr_number alias in user guidance.

attributes.name: estimated_amount
attributes.description: Estimated PR amount used for approval threshold checks.

attributes.name: item_description
attributes.description: Requested item or service description; supports Item/Material semantics only as a line attribute.

attributes.name: target_type
attributes.description: Polymorphic target discriminator.

attributes.name: target_id
attributes.description: Polymorphic target identifier without declared foreign key.

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: DDL includes SKIPPED; sample shows PENDING, APPROVED and REJECTED.

attributes.name: termination_reason
attributes.description: Required by rule when contract is terminated, although nullable structurally in DDL.

attributes.name: po_no
attributes.description: Purchase order number; alias po_number.

attributes.name: po_status
attributes.description: DDL and CSV include CANCELLED; code includes RELEASED, PARTIALLY_RECEIVED and CLOSED; documents mention RELEASED and CLOSED.

attributes.name: receipt_no
attributes.description: Receipt number; CSV values use GRN prefix.

attributes.name: received_by
attributes.description: Receiving user reference; column omits _user_id suffix.

attributes.name: receipt_id
attributes.description: Optional in DDL and often blank for non-matched or exception invoices, but code matching requires a goods receipt object.

attributes.name: invoice_status
attributes.description: Code status constants omit CANCELLED but DDL, CSV and documents include it.

attributes.name: payment_status
attributes.description: Code status constants omit CANCELLED but DDL, CSV and documents include it.

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Approving user reference; column omits _user_id suffix.

relations.description: User belongs to a department via department_id.
Purchase request references the requesting user.
Purchase request is owned by or belongs to a department.
Purchase request may specify a preferred supplier.
Purchase request contains one or more line items before submission.
Approval record references an approver user.
Candidate polymorphic target when target_type is PURCHASE_REQUEST and target_id matches request_id; no declared foreign key.
Candidate polymorphic target when target_type is CONTRACT and target_id matches contract_id; no declared foreign key.
Candidate polymorphic target when target_type is PAYMENT_REQUEST and target_id matches payment_request_id; no declared foreign key.
Contract references the source purchase request.
Contract references one supplier/vendor.
Contract may reference a legal reviewer user.
Purchase order is released or issued under a contract.
Purchase order references the related purchase request while remaining a distinct business document.
Purchase order references the supplier it is issued to.
Goods receipt records receipt against a purchase order.
Goods receipt references the receiving user.
Invoice references its supplier.
Invoice is linked to and matched against a purchase order.
Invoice may be linked to a goods receipt; code matching requires a goods receipt while DDL makes receipt_id optional.
Payment request pays or requests payment for a matched invoice.
Payment request may reference the approving user.

events.name: Purchase Request Created
events.trigger: Requester creates or drafts a purchase request.

events.name: Purchase Request Submitted
events.trigger: Purchase request is submitted for approval.

events.name: Purchase Request Approved
events.trigger: Required approver approves the purchase request.

events.name: Contract Created
events.trigger: Contract is created from an approved purchase request and eligible supplier.

events.name: Purchase Order Released
events.trigger: Purchase order is released under a contract.

events.name: Goods Receipt Recorded
events.trigger: Goods or services are delivered and receipt is recorded.

events.name: Invoice Matched
events.trigger: Invoice is matched against purchase order and goods receipt within policy or implementation tolerance.

events.name: Payment Request Created
events.trigger: Payment application/request is created for a matched invoice.

events.name: Payment Paid
events.trigger: Approved payment request is marked paid.

rules.name: Purchase Request Requires Line Before Submission
rules.condition: Before submitting a purchase request
rules.effect: At least one purchase request line must exist.

rules.name: Business Justification Required Before Submission
rules.condition: Before submitting a purchase request
rules.effect: Business justification must not be blank.

rules.name: Purchase Request Amount Non Negative
rules.condition: Purchase request has an estimated amount
rules.effect: Estimated amount must be greater than or equal to zero.

rules.name: Finance Approval Threshold Document Policy
rules.condition: Purchase request amount is greater than or equal to 50000 CNY
rules.effect: Department Manager approval and then Finance Manager approval are required according to documents and DDL comment.

rules.name: Finance Approval Threshold Code Implementation
rules.condition: PurchaseRequest.estimatedAmount is greater than 50000
rules.effect: ApprovalService.requiredApproverRole returns FINANCE_MANAGER; otherwise it returns DEPARTMENT_MANAGER.

rules.name: Approver Must Match Required Role Or Procurement Admin
rules.condition: Approver role is neither the required approver role nor PROCUREMENT_ADMIN
rules.effect: Approval request is rejected by implementation.

rules.name: Rejection Comment Minimum Length
rules.condition: Rejection comment is null or shorter than 10 characters
rules.effect: Rejecting a request fails in code.

rules.name: Supplier Eligible For Award
rules.condition: Supplier status is ACTIVE and risk rating is not HIGH
rules.effect: Supplier is eligible for award or contract creation.

rules.name: Suspended Supplier Must Not Receive Purchase Orders
rules.condition: Supplier is SUSPENDED
rules.effect: Supplier must not receive purchase orders according to policy.

rules.name: Contract Creation Requires Eligible Supplier
rules.condition: Supplier is null or not eligible for award
rules.effect: Contract creation fails in code.

rules.name: Contract Activation Requires Legal Approval
rules.condition: Contract legal_approved_at is null
rules.effect: Contract activation fails in code; documents also require legal approval before active status.

rules.name: Contract Activation Requires Term
rules.condition: Contract start_date or end_date is null
rules.effect: Contract activation fails in code.

rules.name: Contract Activation Requires Both Signatures In Documents
rules.condition: Contract lacks signatures by both parties
rules.effect: Contract should not be active according to documents.

rules.name: Contract Termination Requires Reason
rules.condition: Contract is terminated or termination is requested
rules.effect: Termination reason is required by code and policy.

rules.name: Purchase Order Release Requires Active Contract
rules.condition: Related contract status is not ACTIVE
rules.effect: Purchase order release fails in code and is disallowed by policy.

rules.name: Purchase Order Close Allowed Statuses In Code
rules.condition: PO status is neither RELEASED nor PARTIALLY_RECEIVED
rules.effect: Closing the purchase order fails in code.

rules.name: Goods Receipt Requires Received Time In Code
rules.condition: Goods receipt received_at is null
rules.effect: Recording goods receipt fails in code.

rules.name: Invoice Matching Requires Goods Receipt
rules.condition: Goods receipt object is null during match
rules.effect: Invoice matching fails in code; documents require receiving evidence for goods invoices.

rules.name: Invoice Match Tolerance Code Implementation
rules.condition: Absolute invoice amount minus PO amount is less than or equal to PO amount multiplied by 0.015
rules.effect: Invoice matching returns true in code.

rules.name: Invoice Variance Tolerance Document Policy
rules.condition: Invoice variance is evaluated against PO amount
rules.effect: Document policy allows variance up to 2 percent unless finance grants an exception.

rules.name: Payment Request Only For Matched Invoice
rules.condition: Related invoice status is not MATCHED
rules.effect: Payment application/request creation fails in code and is disallowed by policy.

rules.name: Payment Approval By Finance Manager Or Procurement Admin
rules.condition: Role code is neither FINANCE_MANAGER nor PROCUREMENT_ADMIN
rules.effect: Payment approval fails in code.

rules.name: Paid Payment Requires Paid Time
rules.condition: Payment request is paid
rules.effect: paid_at or paid time must be stored.

rules.name: Held Payment Requires Hold Reason In Documents
rules.condition: Payment request is HELD
rules.effect: Hold reason is required in operational notes according to document policy.

actions.name: Create Purchase Request
postconditions: Purchase request draft is created or initialized.

actions.name: Submit Purchase Request
postconditions: Purchase request enters SUBMITTED status by business intent.

actions.name: Approve Purchase Request
preconditions: Approver matches threshold-derived role or is procurement admin.

actions.name: Reject Purchase Request
preconditions: Rejection comment is present and code requires at least 10 characters.

actions.name: Draft Contract
preconditions: Purchase request is approved by business intent.

actions.name: Activate Contract
preconditions: Start and end dates exist in code.
Documents require signatures by both parties.

actions.name: Close Purchase Order
preconditions: Code allows closing from RELEASED or PARTIALLY_RECEIVED.
Documents say close may follow complete receipt or admin review.

actions.name: Match Invoice
preconditions: Invoice, purchase order and goods receipt are available.
postconditions: Invoice may become MATCHED or EXCEPTION.

actions.name: Approve Payment Request
preconditions: Role is FINANCE_MANAGER or PROCUREMENT_ADMIN in code.
postconditions: Payment request is APPROVED by business intent.

actions.name: Mark Payment Paid
postconditions: Payment request is PAID and paid_at is populated.

states.name: SKIPPED
states.description: Allowed by DDL but not observed in sample or extracted code/doc rules.

states.name: PARTIALLY_RECEIVED
states.description: Implementation and DDL/CSV state for PO fulfillment; not the same as Goods Receipt PARTIAL.

states.name: CLOSED
states.description: PO lifecycle/fulfillment state; distinct from Goods Receipt COMPLETE.

states.name: CANCELLED
states.description: DDL and CSV support this PO status; code and documents do not clearly implement it.

states.name: PARTIAL
states.description: Goods receipt status; distinct from PO PARTIALLY_RECEIVED.

states.name: COMPLETE
states.description: Goods receipt completion state; distinct from PO CLOSED.

states.name: CANCELLED
states.description: DDL, CSV and documents support this state; code status constants in extraction do not.

states.name: CANCELLED
states.description: DDL, CSV and documents support this state; code status constants in extraction do not.

roles.name: Requester
roles.description: Creates and submits purchase requests; stored code value REQUESTER.

roles.name: Department Manager
roles.description: Approves normal purchase requests; stored role_code value is DEPT_MANAGER.

roles.name: Procurement Specialist
roles.description: Reviews supplier, drafts contracts and releases purchase orders.

roles.name: Finance Manager
roles.description: Approves high-value purchase requests and payment requests/applications.

roles.name: AP Clerk
roles.description: Performs invoice matching and creates payment requests.

roles.name: Procurement Admin
roles.description: Administrative procurement role with override permissions in code and documents.

permissions.condition: Purchase request is editable and required submission fields exist.
Allowed when request amount falls under the department-manager threshold branch; boundary differs by source.
Allowed for high-value PR approval; code uses > 50000 and documents/DDL use >= 50000.
Code allows PROCUREMENT_ADMIN as override approver.
Legal reviewer performs legal review/approval, but activation action is performed by procurement roles in documents.

aliases.source_context: Code uses Supplier domain class but ContractService parameter and message use vendor; documents state supplier/vendor-like meaning.
Code method names use PR; DDL comment and CSV request numbers use PR semantics.
request_no is the purchase request number and PR is the business abbreviation.
Code fields and methods use po; DDL and CSV use po_id, po_no, po_amount and PO number prefix.
po_no represents purchase order number.
CSV receipt_no values use GRN prefix and documents define GRN/Goods Receipt; code lacks GRN text.
receipt_no represents receipt number; CSV values use GRN prefix.
PaymentService.createPaymentApplication returns PaymentRequest; documents call the flow Payment Application.
payment_no represents payment request number; CSV values use PAY prefix.
Code, DDL and CSV contain item_description on PR line, not a separate item master.
Material/Item semantics are treated as purchase request line item_description because no independent material master source object was extracted.
Shared DDL and CSV table prefix retained as naming evidence, not a business concept.
Polymorphic discriminator retained because target relation is not a declared FK.
Polymorphic identifier retained because target relation is not a declared FK.
Stored role code values bridge code constants, DDL enum and CSV samples.
DDL/CSV table name.
DDL/CSV table name.
DDL/CSV table name.
DDL/CSV table name.
DDL/CSV table name.
DDL/CSV table name.
DDL/CSV table name.
DDL/CSV table name.
DDL/CSV table name.
DDL/CSV table name.
DDL/CSV table name.

mappings.transform_note: Supplier/Vendor were merged; Vendor is retained as alias.
Purchase Order/PO was kept separate from Purchase Request/PR despite both carrying request_id.

mappings.source_element: GoodsReceipt, pc_goods_receipt and GRN receipt_no prefix
mappings.transform_note: GRN is merged as alias for Goods Receipt based on documents and CSV number prefix; code lacks GRN string.
Payment Application is merged as alias for Payment Request.
Polymorphic target relations are represented with reduced confidence because DDL lacks foreign keys.

mappings.source_element: Invoice and payment rules tolerance policy
mappings.transform_note: Document tolerance is 2 percent, conflicting with code constant 1.5 percent.

mappings.source_element: DDL comment and document approval policy
mappings.transform_note: DDL and documents use amount greater than or equal to 50000.
Material/Item semantics appear as a line description/category, not as independent material master data.

conflicts.description: Approval threshold boundary differs: code requires FINANCE_MANAGER only when estimatedAmount is greater than 50000, while DDL comment and documents require finance approval for PR amount greater than or equal to 50000.
conflicts.resolution_note: Do not collapse the boundary; preserve both rules until policy owner confirms the intended 50000 case.

conflicts.description: Invoice matching tolerance differs: code uses 0.015 or 1.5 percent while documents state 2 percent tolerance unless finance exception.
conflicts.resolution_note: Keep both implementation and policy values; samples show non-zero variances but do not resolve the policy boundary.

conflicts.description: Documents require both-party signatures before contract activation, but code extraction only checks legal_approved_at plus start/end dates and DDL/CSV have no signature fields.
conflicts.resolution_note: Treat signature requirement as document-only business policy lacking structural implementation evidence.

conflicts.description: Documents require a hold reason for HELD payment, but DDL, code and CSV extracted fields do not include hold_reason or operational_notes on payment_request.
conflicts.resolution_note: Retain as document-only rule with low confidence; no attribute was created because no structural source supports it.

conflicts.description: Policy and code require active contract before PO release, but sample data includes purchase orders released for contracts in LEGAL_REVIEW and DRAFT statuses.
conflicts.resolution_note: Could be sample exception, legacy row, or data quality issue; do not weaken the code/document rule without review.

conflicts.description: Sample payment request 9004 is HELD with approved_by and approved_at populated, while related approval record 5014 is REJECTED.
conflicts.resolution_note: Sample-level inconsistency; preserve as data conflict rather than deriving a rule.

conflicts.description: Invoice CANCELLED status exists in DDL, CSV and documents but not in extracted code status constants.
conflicts.resolution_note: Retain CANCELLED as supported by structure, sample and documents, with note that code implementation is incomplete or absent.

conflicts.description: Payment Request CANCELLED status exists in DDL, CSV and documents but not in extracted code status constants.
conflicts.resolution_note: Retain CANCELLED as supported by structure, sample and documents, with note that code implementation is incomplete or absent.

conflicts.description: Role is named Department Manager in documents and code constant naming, while persisted role_code value is DEPT_MANAGER.
conflicts.resolution_note: Use canonical role Department Manager and retain DEPT_MANAGER as stored role_code value.

uncertainties.description: Approval target relations are polymorphic through target_type and target_id and lack declared DDL foreign keys.
uncertainties.reason: DDL explicitly stores target_type/target_id without target FK constraints; CSV suggests mappings to purchase request, contract and payment request.

uncertainties.description: Signature requirement is document-only and lacks code, DDL and CSV field support.
uncertainties.reason: No extracted contract attribute such as buyer_signature, supplier_signature or signed_at is present.

uncertainties.description: Hold reason policy is supported by documents but no payment_request hold_reason field appears in code, DDL or CSV profile.
uncertainties.reason: Creating attr.payment_request.hold_reason would not be structurally supported by non-document sources.

uncertainties.description: Material/Item semantics appear only as purchase request line item_description/category_code; no independent material or item master concept/table/class was extracted.
uncertainties.reason: Sources support line-level item description, not a standalone material master object.

uncertainties.description: Goods Receipt COMPLETE and Purchase Order CLOSED are intentionally separate owner-scoped states and should not be merged.
uncertainties.reason: DDL and CSV use receipt_status COMPLETE on pc_goods_receipt and po_status CLOSED on pc_purchase_order; documents describe PO close after receipt/admin review.

uncertainties.description: Code extraction reports status constants and service preconditions but some service methods do not explicitly assign resulting statuses.
uncertainties.reason: Events and resulting states partly depend on method names, documents and data rather than explicit code assignments.

uncertainties.description: GRN alias is supported by documents and CSV receipt number prefix but not by extracted code text.
uncertainties.reason: Code concept is GoodsReceipt and no GRN code evidence was extracted.

uncertainties.description: Approval SKIPPED state is DDL-only among extracted results and absent from sample rows and code/doc rules.
uncertainties.reason: Single-source enum value should be retained but with reduced confidence.

uncertainties.description: Purchase Order CANCELLED appears in DDL and CSV but not in extracted code status constants or document status flow.
uncertainties.reason: State is structurally and sample supported but implementation behavior is unknown.

uncertainties.description: Sample includes a MATCHED invoice with non-zero match_variance_amount, which may represent tolerated variance or sample anomaly.
uncertainties.reason: CSV alone cannot determine whether variance is within 1.5 percent code tolerance or 2 percent document tolerance for every row.

uncertainties.description: Some contract and purchase order sample rows reference request_id values absent from purchase request CSV sample.
uncertainties.reason: Could be out-of-sample legacy data or referential inconsistency; DDL still declares FKs.

uncertainties.description: CSV-derived rules such as line_amount equals quantity times unit price and payment PAID implies paid_at are sample patterns, not complete business policy by themselves.
uncertainties.reason: Sample completeness is unknown and candidate patterns may not cover all cases.
